headline: Activision shareholders reach $250 million settlement over Microsoft buyout
summary: Shareholders of Activision Blizzard ‌reached a $250 million settlement ‌over allegations that the company's former executives ​and Microsoft shortchanged them when Microsoft acquired the game maker for $75.4 billion in 2023, according to a ‌late Thursday court ⁠filing in a Delaware state court.  Shareholders in the maker ⁠of the "Call of Duty" video game, led by Swedish pension fund ​Sjunde AP-Fonden, ​accused former ​Activision Blizzard executives ‌including Chief Executive Bobby Kotick of breaching their fiduciary duties to investors by agreeing to a $95 per share takeover price.  The shareholders said ‌Kotick rushed into the ​merger so he ​could keep ​his job and $400 million ‌of change-of-control benefits.

content.title: Activision shareholders reach $250 million settlement over Microsoft buyout
content.summary: Shareholders of Activision Blizzard ‌reached a $250 million settlement ‌over allegations that the company's former executives ​and Microsoft shortchanged them when Microsoft acquired the game maker for $75.4 billion in 2023, according to a ‌late Thursday court ⁠filing in a Delaware state court.  Shareholders in the maker ⁠of the "Call of Duty" video game, led by Swedish pension fund ​Sjunde AP-Fonden, ​accused former ​Activision Blizzard executives ‌including Chief Executive Bobby Kotick of breaching their fiduciary duties to investors by agreeing to a $95 per share takeover price.  The shareholders said ‌Kotick rushed into the ​merger so he ​could keep ​his job and $400 million ‌of change-of-control benefits.

thumbnail.caption: FILE PHOTO: A Microsoft logo is seen in Issy-les-Moulineaux near Paris

headline: Google appeals US court ruling on search monopoly
summary: Alphabet's Google on Friday appealed a Washington federal judge's ruling ‌that it holds illegal monopolies in online search ‌and related advertising.  • Google argued that U.S. ​Judge Amit Mehta made legal errors in his 2024 ruling, which found the company illegally blocked competitors by paying billions of dollars annually to firms including Apple ‌to be the ⁠default search engine on new devices.  • The arrangements did not prevent the device ⁠makers and browser developers from promoting rival search services like Microsoft's Bing, Google argued.

content.title: Google appeals US court ruling on search monopoly
content.summary: Alphabet's Google on Friday appealed a Washington federal judge's ruling ‌that it holds illegal monopolies in online search ‌and related advertising.  • Google argued that U.S. ​Judge Amit Mehta made legal errors in his 2024 ruling, which found the company illegally blocked competitors by paying billions of dollars annually to firms including Apple ‌to be the ⁠default search engine on new devices.  • The arrangements did not prevent the device ⁠makers and browser developers from promoting rival search services like Microsoft's Bing, Google argued.

headline: AI trade: Investors may want to look outside infrastructure, Nvidia
summary: All three of the major averages (^DJI, ^IXIC, ^GSPC) ended Friday's session in positive territory — the Dow Jones Industrial Average leading the way and rising 294 points — managing to close out the trading week with gains. This comes after Nvidia (NVDA) reported first quarter earnings results that beat estimates, but ultimately failed to wow Wall Street investors. Morningstar Wealth CIO for the Americas, Philip Straehl, comes on Market Domination Overtime to assess investor sentiment around the AI trade in response to Nvidia's latest earnings print.
